title: Skill Reputation Tracking in soul_manager
description: Add a lightweight success/failure reputation tracker for learned skills in soul_manager.py. Currently, trust in skills is enforced only at load time via security audit (audit_skill). The reading note's Hawala insight suggests a second trust layer: track each skill's execution outcomes over time, so skills that repeatedly produce good results gain implicit trust, and skills that silently fail get flagged for review — without requiring external enforcement or rule changes.
rationale: The reading note's core claim: in Hawala, reputation IS the infrastructure — not a supplement to rules, but the mechanism itself. Mira currently has rule-based skill trust (security audit + hard rules), but zero reputation-based trust. The note's counterquestion (is Hawala's reliability due to reputation mechanics, or the closed community?) actually resolves in Mira's favor: all of Mira's agents ARE a closed community, so a reputation mechanism should work without the scaling problem. This maps directly to skills: a skill that has been used 40 times successfully should be trusted differently than one used once.
diff_description: In soul_manager.py: (1) Add load_skill_reputation() that reads skill_reputation.json (dict of skill_name -> {uses: int, successes: int, failures: int, last_used: str}). (2) Add record_skill_outcome(skill_name, success: bool) that updates the JSON file. (3) Add get_skill_reputation_score(skill_name) -> float returning successes/uses (default 1.0 for new skills). (4) In the skill recommendation / skill-loading path, log a warning if a skill's reputation score drops below 0.6 after 5+ uses. The skill_reputation.json file starts empty and grows via normal usage. No existing behavior changes — this is additive instrumentation only.
